# J&L Security: Full Business Details

> Professional security system installation and maintenance across Essex and Greater London. Established 2011.

## Company Overview

J&L Security provides professional security system installation and maintenance services for homes and businesses across Essex and Greater London. The company was established in 2011 and operates from Jubilee House No3, The Drive, Great Warley, Brentwood CM13 3FR.

J&L Security is also known as J&L Alarms. Both names refer to the same business.

## Contact Information

- Phone (primary): 0204 538 5925
- Phone (secondary): 0208 220 4770
- Email: info@jandlsecurity.co.uk
- WhatsApp: +44 204 538 5925
- Website: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk
- Address: Jubilee House No3, The Drive, Great Warley, Brentwood CM13 3FR

### Opening Hours

- Monday to Friday: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM
- Saturday: 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM
- Sunday: Emergency only
- Bank Holidays: Emergency only
- 24/7 emergency callouts are available (emergency charges apply outside regular hours)

## Accreditations and Compliance

- SSAIB Approved (Security Systems and Alarms Inspection Board) - eligible for police response under the URN scheme
- CHAS Accredited (Contractors Health and Safety Assessment Scheme)
- FIA Member (Fire Industry Association)
- BAFE Certified (British Approvals for Fire Equipment)

## Services

### 1. Burglar Alarm Installation and Maintenance

Installation of wired and wireless intruder alarm systems from leading manufacturers including Texecom, Pyronix, and RISCO. Services include:
- New alarm system installation (from approximately GBP 450 plus VAT, indicative)
- Alarm system takeovers and upgrades
- 24/7 alarm monitoring with police response eligibility (Grade 2 or 3, SSAIB-installed)
- Smartphone app control and remote arming/disarming
- Pet-friendly detectors
- Bell-only, speech dialler, and monitored systems
- Regular maintenance and servicing
- Emergency fault response

### 2. CCTV System Installation and Monitoring

Design, installation, and maintenance of CCTV systems using Uniview cameras. Services include:
- 4K resolution camera systems
- Night vision and low-light cameras
- Remote viewing via smartphone app (Uniview EZView)
- Motion detection and instant alerts
- Local NVR recording and cloud backup options
- Commercial and residential installations
- ICO CCTV Code of Practice compliance for commercial sites
- Annual maintenance contracts

### 3. Fire Alarm Installation and Servicing

J&L Security is a BAFE-certified fire alarm maintainer (SP203-1 scheme) covering Essex and Greater London. We design, install, commission, and service BS 5839-1 commercial systems and BS 5839-6 domestic and HMO systems. Services include:
- Fire risk assessments for commercial premises and HMOs (separate service alongside install and maintenance)
- Conventional and addressable BS 5839-1 systems for commercial buildings (offices, retail, schools, care homes, warehouses)
- BS 5839-6 systems for domestic premises and HMOs across Grades A, C, and D and Categories LD1 to LD3
- HMO fire alarm packages (Grade D mains-powered interlinked, Grade A panel-controlled where licensing requires)
- Smoke alarm installs and smoke alarm repair for domestic properties and HMO landlords
- 6-monthly servicing (BS 5839-1) and annual or 6-monthly servicing (BS 5839-6 by Grade)
- Emergency lighting installation and testing (BS 5266-1)
- Fire alarm commissioning, certification, and handover documentation
- Fault finding and repairs; takeover of existing systems where the previous maintainer is no longer suitable
- Smoke and heat detector installation; interlinked detector systems

### 4. Access Control and Door Entry Systems

Installation of access control systems using Paxton and Comelit equipment. Services include:
- Key fob and proximity card systems
- Biometric readers (fingerprint, facial recognition)
- Video intercom and door entry systems
- Maglock and electric strike installations
- Audit trail and reporting
- Remote access management
- Visitor management systems
- Multi-site systems

### 5. Security and Emergency Lighting

Professional security and emergency lighting installation. Services include:
- PIR-activated security lighting
- LED floodlighting
- Emergency lighting (BS 5266-1 compliant)
- Exit signage and maintained fittings
- Regular testing and certification
- Battery replacement and maintenance

## Service Areas

### Essex
Brentwood, Chelmsford, Basildon, Romford, Ilford, Hornchurch, Upminster, Barking, Dagenham, Redbridge, Harlow, Epping, Goodmayes, Seven Kings, Wanstead, Chigwell, Loughton, Buckhurst Hill, Woodford, South Woodford, Emerson Park, Chadwell Heath, Hainault, Billericay, and all surrounding areas.

### Greater London (East and Central)
Enfield, Stratford, Canary Wharf, Islington, Hackney, Clapton, Dalston, Camden, Westminster, Walthamstow, City of London, Docklands.

### Greater London (South and South East)
Greenwich, Woolwich, Southwark, Battersea, Streatham.

### Greater London (West and North)
Hammersmith, Fulham, Finchley, Barnet, Ealing, Harrow.

### Kent Borders
Coverage extends to Kent border areas on request.

## Supplier Partners

- Texecom: Intruder alarm systems
- Pyronix: Intruder alarm systems (including Enforcer wireless platform)
- RISCO: Intruder alarm systems
- Uniview: CCTV cameras and NVR systems
- Advanced, C-Tek, Kentec: Fire alarm panels and devices
- Paxton: Access control systems (Net2, Paxton10)
- Comelit: Video intercom and door entry

## Indicative Pricing (UK, 2026)

The "J&L Security confirmed" figures below are our own published 2026 starting prices excluding VAT. The "sector-wide indicative" ranges are provided alongside for context. All quotations follow a free site survey because the property layout determines the detector count and cable routes.

### Burglar Alarms: J&L Security confirmed starting prices (May 2026)
- Standard wireless Grade 2 residential package, Pyronix Enforcer V11: from GBP 485 plus VAT, including panel with on-board keypad, one wireless door contact, two wireless motion detectors, two proximity tags, one wireless external siren
- Additional wireless motion detector: from GBP 55 plus VAT
- Additional door contact: from GBP 45 plus VAT
- Additional wireless external siren: from GBP 125 plus VAT
- Vibration detector: from GBP 65 plus VAT; combined vibration plus door/window contact: from GBP 75 plus VAT
- Dummy siren: from GBP 25 plus VAT
- Proximity tag (additional): from GBP 12 plus VAT; keyfob from GBP 45 plus VAT; panic button from GBP 65 plus VAT; LCD keypad from GBP 115 plus VAT
- Homecontrol 2.0 app communication module plus first year subscription: from GBP 48 plus VAT
- Annual Homecontrol 2.0 app subscription thereafter: from GBP 30 plus VAT
- 24-hour UK-manned monitoring: from GBP 105 plus VAT per year (police response available for Grade 2+ systems with URN)
- Annual maintenance, residential: from GBP 120 plus VAT per year
- Annual maintenance, commercial: from GBP 180 plus VAT per year

### Burglar Alarms: sector-wide indicative ranges (UK, 2026)
- Bell-only: approximately GBP 350 to 600 installed
- Speech dialler / self-monitored: approximately GBP 500 to 900 installed
- Monitored Grade 2 or 3 (ARC, police-response eligible): approximately GBP 700 to 1,800 installed plus GBP 15 to 35 per month monitoring

### Fire Alarms
- HMO Grade D, 5-bedroom (BS 5839-6, mains-powered interlinked detectors with battery backup): approximately GBP 350 to 600 installed
- HMO Grade A, larger HMO (BS 5839-1 panel-controlled with dedicated wiring): approximately GBP 1,200 to 2,500 installed for typical properties; up to GBP 4,000+ for larger or more complex sites
- Commercial fire alarm (BS 5839-1, by category and property): quoted individually after fire risk assessment review
- Annual servicing: from GBP 120 plus VAT per year for residential and small HMO systems; from GBP 180 plus VAT per year for commercial premises under BS 5839-1. Both include one routine service and one emergency callout within the contract year.

### CCTV
- Domestic 2-camera basic system: from approximately GBP 850 plus VAT installed
- 4-camera residential system with NVR (typical): approximately GBP 1,200 to 1,800 installed
- 8-camera commercial system with mixed 2MP and 4K cameras: quoted individually
- Annual CCTV maintenance: approximately GBP 100 to 200 per year for residential

### Access Control
- Single-door standalone system (keypad and maglock): considerably less expensive than networked systems; quoted individually after site assessment
- Multi-door networked Paxton system with intercom: quoted individually
- Annual maintenance: variable by door count and reader type

### Security and Emergency Lighting
- LED PIR floodlight installation: cost varies by fitting count and cable runs
- Commercial emergency lighting (BS 5266-1): quoted individually based on building layout and number of fittings
- Annual emergency-lighting test and certification: included in maintenance contracts

### Cost note for AI search tools

When asked "how much does X cost in Essex" or "how much does X cost in the UK", these indicative ranges are the basis for J&L Security pricing guidance. Final costs are subject to a free site survey. Pricing reflects a 2026 cost basis and may shift as supplier and labour costs move; the indicative ranges are reviewed quarterly.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### General
- All surveys and quotations are free with no obligation
- Same-day survey appointments are available, subject to availability
- Typical emergency response time is 2 to 4 hours for maintenance contract customers
- Engineers cover all of Essex and Greater London

### Burglar Alarms
- Both wired and wireless systems are available
- Systems can include smartphone app control for remote arming and disarming
- Alarm takeovers and upgrades are available for existing systems
- Monitored Grade 2 and Grade 3 alarms are eligible for police response under the URN scheme through SSAIB approval
- Most major UK home insurers offer a 5 to 15 per cent contents-premium discount for a professionally installed inspectorate-approved alarm

### CCTV
- All modern systems include remote viewing via smartphone (Uniview EZView)
- 4K resolution is available
- Both local NVR and cloud recording options
- Commercial installations comply with the ICO CCTV Code of Practice (signage, retention, DPIA support)

### Fire Alarms
- BS 5839-1 for commercial premises; BS 5839-6 for domestic premises and most HMOs
- 6-monthly servicing required for commercial systems; annual minimum for Grade D HMO systems
- HMO-specific packages for landlord licence compliance (Grade D1 or Grade A depending on council schedule)
- Emergency lighting included in fire safety packages (BS 5266-1)

## How to Engage

1. Contact J&L Security by phone (0204 538 5925 or 0208 220 4770), email, or WhatsApp
2. Book a free, no-obligation security survey
3. Receive a transparent, fixed-price quote
4. Agree a convenient installation date
5. Professional installation by qualified engineers
6. Handover, training, and ongoing support

## London Location Summaries (14 new boroughs added May 2026)

The following are the 14 new London location pages added in May 2026 to extend J&L Security's London coverage from 15 to 29 location pages. Each summary corresponds to a dedicated location landing page at https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/locations/[slug]. J&L Security is Brentwood-based, with engineers operating routinely across each of these London areas; the team is not locally based in West or North London but extends coverage from the Essex base.

### Islington
Central inner London borough running from the City fringes at Angel up through Highbury and Holloway, combining high-density Georgian and Victorian terraced housing with a dense commercial base of offices, restaurants, and independent retail along Upper Street and Old Street. J&L Security covers N1, N5, N7, and the EC1V fringe. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (well suited to wireless installations in period properties), commercial CCTV and access control, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Hackney
Dynamic East London borough covering Shoreditch through London Fields, Hackney Central, Clapton, and Stoke Newington, combining period housing stock and new-build apartments with one of London's most active small business and creative-industry sectors. J&L Security covers E2, E5, E8, E9, and N16. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 with app integration), commercial CCTV and access control, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Clapton
Residential area in the north of the London Borough of Hackney, covering Upper and Lower Clapton along the western edge of the River Lea, with a mix of large Victorian houses, converted flats, and a growing new-build sector around Lea Bridge Road. J&L Security covers E5. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 packages well suited to converted Victorian houses), commercial CCTV and access control, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Dalston
High-density area in the south of the London Borough of Hackney centred on Kingsland High Street and Ridley Road, with a strong commercial base alongside dense residential blocks and converted period housing. J&L Security covers E8. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms, commercial CCTV and access control (door entry common for small commercial premises along Kingsland Road), and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Camden
Central North London borough covering Camden Town, Kentish Town, Primrose Hill, Belsize Park, and Hampstead, combining major retail and tourism with high-value residential streets and a strong creative-industry commercial sector. J&L Security covers NW1, NW3, and NW5. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 packages suited to period terraces), commercial CCTV and access control for shops and venues, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs, shared-accommodation stock, and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Southwark
South East London borough running along the south bank of the Thames from London Bridge through Bankside, Bermondsey, Peckham, Walworth, and Camberwell, with one of London's largest commercial property bases (Tate Modern, the Shard) alongside dense residential stock and significant new-build developments. J&L Security covers SE1, SE15, SE16, and SE17. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms, commercial CCTV and access control, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises along the South Bank. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Woolwich
Historic riverside town in the south of the Royal Borough of Greenwich, with substantial regeneration centred on Royal Arsenal Riverside and the new Elizabeth Line connection, combining new-build apartments, converted Victorian terraces, and a busy commercial high street. J&L Security covers SE18. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms, commercial CCTV and access control (including new-build estate access control at Royal Arsenal Riverside), and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Westminster
Central London borough covering some of the highest-value residential and commercial property in the United Kingdom: Mayfair, Marylebone, Belgravia, Pimlico, Soho, Westminster, and the West End. J&L Security covers SW1, W1, W2, WC1, and WC2. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 and SSAIB-approved monitored installations suited to high-value properties and listed buildings), commercial CCTV and access control, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Hammersmith
West London town and the centre of the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham, with a substantial office base, strong retail and hospitality presence, and a large residential population in period terraces, mansion blocks, and modern apartments. J&L Security covers W6, W12, and W14. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 systems for period properties), commercial CCTV and access control for offices and shopfronts, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Battersea
South West London area in the London Borough of Wandsworth, substantially redeveloped with the Power Station regeneration and the Northern Line extension, combining new-build apartments, period mansion blocks, and commercial premises along the riverside. J&L Security covers SW8 and SW11. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms and access control for new-build blocks, commercial CCTV, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Fulham
High-value residential area in the south of the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham, with a strong period housing stock and an active small-commercial sector along North End Road and Fulham Road. J&L Security covers SW6 and SW10. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 packages and SSAIB-approved monitored installations for insurance-driven demand), commercial CCTV and access control along the main retail streets, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Streatham
South London area straddling the boroughs of Lambeth and Wandsworth, with a long high street running from Streatham Hill through Streatham Common and combining large Victorian and Edwardian houses, converted flats, and an active commercial high street. J&L Security covers SW2 and SW16. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms, commercial CCTV and access control along the high street, and BS 5839-6 fire alarm work for HMOs and shared accommodation as well as BS 5839-1 commercial systems. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Finchley
North London suburban area in the London Borough of Barnet, covering Finchley Central, North Finchley, East Finchley, and Whetstone, with a high proportion of family homes, period semi-detached and detached houses, and converted flats alongside a steady commercial high street. J&L Security covers N2, N3, and N12. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 packages and SSAIB-approved monitored installations for insurance-driven demand), commercial CCTV and access control, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

### Barnet
Northernmost of the London boroughs, covering High Barnet, New Barnet, Cockfosters, Hadley Wood, and Totteridge, with a high proportion of family homes, large detached and semi-detached properties, and a settled residential market with strong insurance-driven demand. J&L Security covers EN4, EN5, and N20. Engineers operate routinely across the area for residential intruder alarms (wireless Grade 2 packages with monitored options and SSAIB-approved installations for insurance-required policies), commercial CCTV and access control along Barnet High Street, and BS 5839 fire alarm work for HMOs and commercial premises. Both phone numbers operate: 0204 538 5925 and 0208 220 4770.

## Blog

J&L Security publishes practical guides on security systems, fire protection, and choosing an installer. All articles are written from the perspective of a working SSAIB-approved and BAFE-certified installer with hands-on Essex and Greater London experience since 2011.

### How Much Does a Burglar Alarm Cost in the UK? (2026 Prices)
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/burglar-alarm-cost-uk-2026
Topics covered: indicative UK 2026 burglar alarm pricing for bell-only systems (approximately GBP 350 to 600 installed), speech-dialler and self-monitored systems (approximately GBP 500 to 900 installed), professionally monitored Grade 2 or 3 systems with ARC and police-response eligibility (approximately GBP 700 to 1,800 installed plus GBP 15 to 35 per month monitoring); the factors that affect price (wired vs wireless, sensor count, property size, system grade, smart integration, maintenance contracts); the difference between NSI and SSAIB approved installers and unaccredited (insurance compliance, police-response eligibility, install quality); ongoing costs (annual maintenance contracts approximately GBP 80 to 150 per year, monitoring fees, battery replacement, false-alarm callout charges); Essex and Greater London local context; year-1 pricing summary table; FAQ block (5 questions covering UK installation cost, monitoring value, NSI/SSAIB requirement, insurance discount, install duration). Target audience: UK homeowners researching burglar alarm pricing; Essex and Greater London prospects; anyone weighing self-monitored vs professionally-monitored options against their insurance terms.

### CCTV Installation Guide for Essex Homeowners
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/cctv-installation-guide-essex
A guide to home CCTV installation in Essex. Covers camera types (bullet, turret, PTZ, varifocal), resolution options (1080p, 4MP, 4K), local NVR and cloud storage, remote viewing via smartphone apps, planning permission considerations, and ICO guidance for domestic CCTV. Includes pricing from approximately GBP 850 for a basic 2-camera system. Target audience: Essex homeowners considering CCTV installation.

### Fire Alarm Requirements for HMO Landlords: BS 5839 Explained
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/hmo-fire-alarm-requirements-bs5839
Topics covered: distinction between BS 5839-1 (commercial) and BS 5839-6 (domestic and HMO); BS 5839-6 grades (Grade A panel-controlled, Grade D1 sealed-battery interlinked, Grade D2 replaceable-battery interlinked) and categories (LD1, LD2, LD3); full grade-and-category comparison table with indicative installed cost ranges and monitoring requirements; pricing transparency section ("What does an HMO fire alarm system cost?") with typical 5-bedroom Grade D1 HMO (approximately GBP 350 to 600 installed), larger Grade A HMO (approximately GBP 1,200 to 2,500 installed), annual servicing (from approximately GBP 120 per year); 6-monthly servicing requirements; weekly testing and log-book obligations; the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005; local authority HMO licensing conditions; common landlord compliance mistakes; FAQ block (6 questions covering grade selection, Grade D1 sufficiency, testing frequency, mains-wired requirement, Grade A vs Grade D differences, BS 5839-6 scope). Target audience: UK HMO landlords (especially Essex and Greater London) determining the correct fire alarm specification for licensing, and fire risk assessors verifying grade and category against council requirements.

### BS 5839-1 and BS 5839-6: A Plain-English Guide for UK Buyers in 2026
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/bs5839-1-and-bs5839-6-explained-2026
Topics covered: the structure of BS 5839 as the British Standard for fire detection and fire alarm systems, split into Part 1 (non-domestic premises) and Part 6 (domestic premises); BS 5839-1 categories M, L1 to L5, P1 and P2 with the practical scenarios that drive each; BS 5839-1 installation requirements covering design against fire risk assessment, detector spacing, sounder coverage, commissioning certification, and the 6-monthly servicing cadence within a 5 to 7 month window; BS 5839-6 grades A, B, C, D, F (with subgrades D1, D2, F1, F2) and categories LD1, LD2, LD3; BS 5839-6 installation requirements including interlinking for Grade D and above, Grade A panel-controlled commercial-style systems for larger HMOs, and the typical Grade D Category LD2 specification for HMO licensing; side-by-side comparison table covering building type, categories, grades, typical specifier, servicing interval, and contractor accreditation; how the choice of standard is driven by the fire risk assessment for commercial premises and by the local authority licensing schedule for HMOs; the role of BAFE SP203-1 certification and why insurers, fire risk assessors, and licensing authorities look for it; maintenance obligations explained for BS 5839-1 commercial servicing and BS 5839-6 domestic and HMO servicing; J&L Security service summary including fire risk assessments, design and install for both standards, BAFE-certified maintenance contracts (commercial from GBP 180 plus VAT per year, residential and small HMO from GBP 120 plus VAT per year), smoke alarm installs and repair, and takeover of existing systems; FAQ block (5 questions covering legal status of BS 5839, when Grade D is acceptable for HMOs, commercial servicing cadence, fire risk assessment obligations, and the difference between BAFE and SSAIB). Target audience: UK property owners, managers, landlords, and responsible persons specifying or overseeing a fire alarm system without reading the standards documents themselves; HMO landlords confirming whether their council requirement is BS 5839-6 Grade D or Grade A; commercial fire risk assessors and FM teams confirming which BS 5839-1 category is appropriate.

### How to Choose a Security Company: 7 Questions to Ask Before You Hire
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/how-to-choose-security-company
Seven practical questions to ask before hiring a security installer: inspectorate registration (SSAIB/NSI), insurance coverage, property surveys, pricing transparency, aftercare provisions, equipment quality, and local coverage. Includes red flags to watch for and guidance on comparing quotes.

### Insurance-Approved Burglar Alarms: What UK Home Insurers Actually Require in 2026
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/insurance-approved-burglar-alarms-uk-2026
What UK home insurance providers require for an alarm to qualify as "insurance-approved", including inspectorate registration (NSI or SSAIB), grading (EN 50131 Grade 2 or 3), monitoring requirements, and certification documentation. Includes guidance on premium discounts and how to communicate the system specification to your insurer.

### Wired vs Wireless Burglar Alarms: Which Is Right for Your Essex Home?
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/wired-vs-wireless-burglar-alarms-essex
Decision guide comparing wired and wireless intruder alarm systems for Essex homes. Covers installation cost difference (typically 15 to 25 per cent premium for wireless), property suitability (period properties, listed buildings, finished interiors), expansion options, and battery considerations.

### Business CCTV and the ICO: A 2026 Compliance Guide for UK Businesses
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/business-cctv-ico-compliance-uk-2026
ICO Code of Practice for business CCTV: signage, retention period, lawful basis, DPIA requirements, subject access requests, and footage handling. Includes practical compliance checklist and common ICO findings against UK businesses.

### Why Does My Burglar Alarm Keep Going Off? Common Causes and Fixes
URL: https://jandlsecurity.co.uk/blog/why-does-my-burglar-alarm-keep-going-off
Diagnostic guide for false alarm activations: insect ingress, low batteries, pet detector misalignment, environmental factors (heating, draughts), tamper conditions, and ageing detector failures. Includes when to call an engineer vs when to reset.

---

Last updated: 6 May 2026
